title: Thunderbird will not let me delete all the junk from my Junk folder.
creator: DanRaymer
content:
Starting yesterday, Thunderbird will not let me delete all the junk from my Junk folder. When I right click on it and select Delete, T-bird freezes with NotResponding and I have to restart it. Once I went through and used Delete on the individual files within the Junk Folder. When I restarted T-bird, it had moved all those files to my Inbox - several thousand of them.

Can I simply delete the Junk file at \AppData\Roaming\Thunderbird\Profiles\hxza0a1k.default-release\Mail\mail.aircraftdesign.com? Or would that kill the whole thing?

Any other workaround? I spent 4 hours talking to Network Solutions, they checked all my account settings and said it is all correct.

title: Tasks and start dates

content: hi all. if i create a new blank Task it automatically pre-selects today's date as the start date, and if i create a Task from an email it sets the date the email was received as the start date. i know can filter tasks lists, and hide columns, but the real issue is how it looks on the calendar; with the fixed start date, setting a due date in a month's time results in a fixed, ongoing task for a month. is there any plugin or setting that will just stop TB setting a start date that i can't change, or letting me explicitly set it myself?

title: downolading files from rss channels is broken
creator: _Petr_
content: Hi, The goal is download attachments from a RSS feed. I set a specific folder so i don't have to select path for every single file. Then clicking on the file link and it should start to download but it is very unreliable especially when used on many files in sequence it silently fails. Related big problem is absence on any useful file transfer indicator like Firefox has. There is a "download manager" tab but it is completely useless. It does not show any progress and the view has no scroll bar so nothing useful can actually be seen there. Downloading using "save as" button works. So you should either fix the direct link download or disable the feature completely until it does work, to avid missing files, because that is the worst outcome.

title: Getting a message about being blacklisted
creator: michael.butler101
content:
I have two different email accounts in Thunderbird. One is a Gmail account one is from Comcast.net. When I start Thunderbird it checks Gmail fine, but then I get a little pop up in the lower left-hand corner it says Comcast has blacklisted my IP. After a few more seconds a dialogue box pops up asking for my password. When I type in my password I get the same crap over and over.

I know my IP is not blacklisted because I can log on to my Comcast account in a browser no problem same IP. What's going on, do you think I picked up some sort of malware? I've scanned I can't find anything.

Michael Butler michael.butler101@comcast.net

title: Sending of Password fails
creator: kendepue
content:
As so many have experienced, but I have yet to find the actual solution, I will bring up the issue.

I recently purchased a new computer. Installed Thunderbird, created all the mail accounts I have and... no problems. All the accounts, once created, populated with emails. For 3-4 days, there were no issues.

Important to note: I let TB use all the default settings. And once everything was up and running >>>>I did not change anything<<<<<.

Then a few days later, I started getting the following messages for two accounts that had migrated from q.com to myctl.net. Entering the 100% proven-correct password results in the know endless-loop of entering the password and the same pop-up error message. The PW is 100% correct as I can go to Centurylinks' website and login using that same, exact password and read my emails.

I've spent enough time trying all the suggestions here, reddit, facebook etc so I'd thought I'd see if anyone has actually found a solution.

title: Login to Server imap.comcast.net with Username Failed
creator: Ferrell516
content:
I have the latest Thunderbird update. Since yesterday, using imap.comcast.net, I have been getting the error "Login to server imap.comcast.net with username [removed] failed." It wants me to retry or enter a new password. I cannot get emails. I can, however, log into my Comcast email account online and see emails delivered. Thunderbird, of course, will not receive these emails.

For imap.comcast.net, port is 993, connection security is SSL/TLS, and authentication method is normal password. I have not changed any of these settings. I have not changed my password. I have done nothing to cause the problem.

I cannot find a solution online. Could someone help? Thank you!
